description: Lightweight qualitative output suite for the execution-briefing skill. These cases are meant for human review after running the skill on representative run-brief drafting, quick-lane, approval-checkpoint, and route-back tasks.

assertion_definitions.run_goal_clear: The output defines a concrete goal for the current run rather than restating the entire issue at a high level.
assertion_definitions.non_goals_explicit: The brief clearly states what this run must not expand into.
assertion_definitions.checks_and_evidence_usable: Required checks and required evidence are concrete enough for downstream verification.
assertion_definitions.risks_and_blockers_visible: Dependencies, blockers, or execution risks are named instead of hidden.
assertion_definitions.done_contract_concrete: The done contract includes observable outcomes, checks, and evidence rather than vague completion language.
assertion_definitions.routing_honest: When the work is not ready for a brief, the output routes to shaping, clarify, exploration, or planning instead of pretending otherwise.
assertion_definitions.stays_lighter_than_plan: The artifact remains a compact run brief and does not expand into a deep multi-phase implementation plan.

cases.name: Turn a shaped issue into a usable run brief
cases.repo_archetype: Engineering repo with issue-driven execution and structured verification
cases.prompt: Take this shaped issue and draft the run brief plus done contract for the next execution attempt.
success_criteria: Defines one concrete run goal and explicit non-goals.
Turns issue acceptance into checks and evidence expectations.
Names a credible starting path without becoming a full plan.

cases.name: A small stable issue can use the quick lane without losing rigor
cases.repo_archetype: Repo with a small, already-shaped bug fix and straightforward verification
cases.prompt: Produce a quick execution brief for a compact shaped issue whose run goal, non-goals, and likely starting surface are already obvious.
success_criteria: Uses a shorter artifact shape without becoming vague.
Still defines checks, evidence, and escalation triggers.
Does not drift into deep planning or direct implementation.

cases.name: A brief can be ready while execution is intentionally held for sign-off
cases.repo_archetype: Repo with an explicit approval checkpoint before implementation starts
cases.prompt: Draft the execution brief and done contract, then hold for approval before the execution owner starts work.
success_criteria: Produces a usable brief rather than only a vague request for approval.
Makes the approval hold explicit and keeps execution paused.
Still names checks, evidence, and next-step ownership.

cases.name: Produce a brief for a task with clear verification and gate pressure
cases.repo_archetype: Repo where review, browser QA, or merge gates matter before completion
cases.prompt: Draft an execution brief for a shaped issue that has review and browser QA gate expectations before merge.
success_criteria: Makes required checks, required evidence, and gate expectations explicit.
Names the downstream gate source of truth when it matters.
Names the main risks or blockers up front.
Keeps the brief compact and execution-focused.

cases.name: Large cross-cutting work should route to planning honestly
cases.repo_archetype: Monorepo with multi-service migrations and staged rollout constraints
cases.prompt: Create the execution brief for a cross-service migration that clearly needs sequencing, rollback design, and team coordination.
success_criteria: Does not fake readiness for a compact run brief.
Explains why the work needs deeper planning.
Points to the smallest honest next workflow.
